content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Here are the answers for all seven steps.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What it is:** `ruby_llm` — "One delightful Ruby framework for every major AI provider" (a Rails-aware gem, currently in 2.0 development). 296 files, code lives under `lib/ruby_llm/`.

**Key architectural split (this is the spine of everything below):**
- **`RubyLLM::Provider`** (`lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:10`) — knows *where* to talk: host/`api_base`, `headers`, auth, config, and *which* protocol to speak. One concrete subclass per external service in `lib/ruby_llm/providers/*.rb` (anthropic, azure, bedrock, deepseek, gemini, gpustack, mistral, ollama, openai, openrouter, perplexity, vertexai, xai), each with a sibling `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<name>/` dir holding its customization modules + `capabilities.rb`.
- **`RubyLLM::Protocol`** (`lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb:7`) — knows *how* to talk: renders payloads, parses responses, streams, endpoints. The wire dialects live in `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/`: `chat_completions` (the OpenAI lingua franca), `responses`, `anthropic`, `gemini`, `converse` (Bedrock) — each a `.rb` class + a dir of mixin modules (chat, media, tools, streaming, models, embeddings…).

**User-facing surface:** `RubyLLM.chat/embed/paint/...` (`lib/ruby_llm.rb:58`) → `RubyLLM::Chat` (`lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:7`). Supporting machinery: `Models` registry (`lib/ruby_llm/models.rb`, catalog `models.json`, aliases `aliases.json`), `Configuration` (`lib/ruby_llm/configuration.rb`), `Connection` (Faraday, `lib/ruby_llm/connection.rb`), `Error`/`ErrorMiddleware` (`lib/ruby_llm/error.rb`, `error_middleware.rb`). Registration is centralized in `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116`; autoloading via Zeitwerk (`lib/ruby_llm.rb:17-39`).

---

## Step 2 — Request → concrete service object

Two phases: **provider selection** (at model binding) and **protocol selection** (at completion).

1. `RubyLLM.chat(...)` → `Chat.new` — `lib/ruby_llm.rb:58` → `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:12`
2. `Chat#initialize` → `with_model` — `chat.rb:20`, defined `chat.rb:84`
3. `with_model` → `Models.resolve(model_id, provider:, assume_exists:, config:)` — `chat.rb:85`
4. `Models.resolve` — `models.rb:132-156`: the lookup.
   - explicit provider → `Provider.providers[provider.to_sym]` (`models.rb:134`)
   - otherwise → `Models.find` then `Provider.providers[model.provider.to_sym]` (`models.rb:152-153`)
   - returns `[model, provider_class.new(config)]` (`models.rb:155`) — **instantiates the concrete Provider object**
5. `Provider#initialize` → `ensure_configured!` + builds `Connection` — `provider.rb:13-17`
6. The lookup table itself: `Provider.providers` hash (`provider.rb:183`), populated by `Provider.register` (`provider.rb:174`), seeded at `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116`. `Model::Info#provider_class` (`model/info.rb:107`) resolves the same way.

Then per request:
7. `chat.ask` → `complete` → `complete_once` → `provider_completion` → `@provider.complete(...)` — `chat.rb:41`, `179`, `278`, `295-310`
8. `Provider#complete` — `provider.rb:54-69`: calls `resolve_protocol(...)` (`provider.rb:56`) then `protocol_class.new(self, model).complete(...)`
9. `resolve_protocol` — `provider.rb:210-213`: `explicit = name || configured_protocol`; explicit → `fetch_protocol`, else `protocol_for(model, **request)`
   - `protocol_for` default → `default_protocol` (`provider.rb:49-51`); e.g. OpenAI overrides to route audio/realtime models (`openai.rb:15`)
   - `fetch_protocol` reads the `protocols` hash (`provider.rb:223`) filled by the class-level `protocol` declaration (`provider.rb:163`, e.g. `openai.rb:7-8`)
10. `Protocol#complete` runs the wire work → `sync_response`/`stream_response` → `completion_url` + `render_payload` + `parse_completion_response` — `protocol.rb:20-44`, `109-114`

---

## Step 3 — The shared dialect (ChatCompletions) and every customization

**Shared base:** `Protocols::ChatCompletions < Protocol` (`protocols/chat_completions.rb:6`). It `include`s nine modules (`chat_completions.rb:7-15`), each in `protocols/chat_completions/`: `Chat` (`chat.rb` — `completion_url`, `render_payload`, `parse_completion_response`, `format_messages`, `format_role`, token accounting, citations, thinking extraction), `Embeddings`, `Models`, `Moderation`, `Streaming`, `Tools`, `Images`, `Media`, `Transcription`. The base `Protocol` (`protocol.rb`) supplies the `complete/list_models/embed/moderate/paint/transcribe` orchestration. Default temperature normalization is overridden here (`chat_completions.rb:17`).

**Customization mechanism:** a provider declares an inner `class ChatCompletions < Protocols::ChatCompletions` and `include`s provider modules that override the `module_function` methods; plus provider-level overrides of `api_base`, `headers`, `parse_error`, `protocol_for`, and class methods `capabilities`/`configuration_*`/`local?`.

**Every existing ChatCompletions integration and how it differs:**

| Provider | File | Customizations |
|---|---|---|
| **OpenAI** | `openai.rb` | Canonical. Also offers `Protocols::Responses` (`:7-8`); `protocol_for` routes `audio/realtime/search-preview` models to chat_completions (`:15-17`); `OpenAI::Capabilities`. |
| **DeepSeek** | `deepseek.rb:8-12` | `DeepSeek::Chat` (`deepseek/chat.rb`): `format_role` (no `developer` role), `format_content` (text-only, no docs/images/audio). `DeepSeek::Capabilities`. |
| **Perplexity** | `perplexity.rb:8-11` | `Perplexity::Chat`, `Perplexity::Models`, `perplexity/media.rb`; **provider-level `parse_error` override** for HTML auth errors (`perplexity.rb:26-40`). |
| **xAI** | `xai.rb:8-11` | `XAI::Chat` (`format_role`, image-only `format_content` — `xai/chat.rb`), `XAI::Models` (custom `parse_list_models_response` — `xai/models.rb`). |
| **Mistral** | `mistral.rb:8-13` | `Mistral::Chat` (overrides `render_payload` via `super` — deletes `stream_options`, configures thinking, normalizes `tool_choice` `any`/required — `mistral/chat.rb:26-52`), `Mistral::Embeddings` (custom URL/payload/parse), `Mistral::Media` (`document_url`), `Mistral::Models`; `Mistral::Capabilities`. |
| **Ollama** | `ollama.rb:8-12` | `Ollama::Chat`, `Media`, `Models`; `local? = true` (`:35`); `api_base` required, key optional. |
| **OpenRouter** | `openrouter.rb:8-13` | `OpenRouter::Chat`, `Images`, `Models`, **`Streaming`**; **provider-level `parse_error`** for nested `error.metadata.raw` (`openrouter.rb:27-64`). |
| **GPUStack** | `gpustack.rb:8-9` | Subclasses **`Ollama::ChatCompletions`** (not the base), adds `GPUStack::Models`; `local? = true`. |

**Customization points to know (a missed one = silently unsupported):** `format_role`, `format_content`/`Media`, `render_payload` (super+tweak), `build_tool_choice`, `parse_completion_response`, `parse_list_models_response` (Models), embeddings trio (`embedding_url`/`render_embedding_payload`/`parse_embedding_response`), `Streaming`, `Images`, and provider-level `api_base`/`headers`/`parse_error`/`protocol_for`.

---

## Step 4 — Settings: declare → register → validate

- **Declare** (per provider class): `configuration_options` (all keys to expose) and `configuration_requirements` (must-be-present keys). E.g. `openai.rb:32-44`, `deepseek.rb:29-35`, `ollama.rb:27-33`.
- **Register**: `Provider.register(name, class)` (`provider.rb:174-177`) calls `RubyLLM::Configuration.register_provider_options(provider_class.configuration_options + [:"#{name}_protocol"])`. → `register_provider_options` (`configuration.rb:25-27`) → `option` (`configuration.rb:10-23`) which defines an `attr_reader` + a blank-stripping writer on `Configuration`. So **registering the provider auto-creates its config accessors** — no manual edit of `configuration.rb`.
- **Validate**:
  - `Provider.configured?(config)` (`provider.rb:159-161`) = all requirements truthy.
  - `Provider#ensure_configured!` (`provider.rb:237-249`) runs at instantiation (`provider.rb:15`) and raises `ConfigurationError` with a generated config snippet if any requirement is missing.
  - `Provider#configured?` (`provider.rb:92-94`); fleet-level filters `configured_providers`/`configured_remote_providers` (`provider.rb:195-205`).

---

## Step 5 — HTTP failures → typed errors

- **Wired** in Faraday stack: `faraday.use :llm_errors, provider: @provider` — `connection.rb:117`; middleware registered at `error_middleware.rb:90`.
- **Entry**: `ErrorMiddleware#call` → `on_complete` → `self.class.parse_error(provider:, response:)` — `error_middleware.rb:14-18`.
- **Classification**: `ErrorMiddleware.parse_error` — `error_middleware.rb:41-71`:
  1. `message = provider&.parse_error(response)` (`:42`) — provider extracts the human message.
  2. `case response.status` maps codes → typed errors: 400→`BadRequestError`/`ContextLengthExceededError`, 401→`UnauthorizedError`, 402→`PaymentRequiredError`, 403→`ForbiddenError`, 429→`RateLimitError`/`ContextLengthExceededError`, 500→`ServerError`, 502-504→`ServiceUnavailableError`, 529→`OverloadedError`, else→`Error` (`:44-70`).
  - Heuristics `context_length_exceeded?`/`rate_limited?` via regex tables (`error_middleware.rb:21-39`, `75-85`).
- **Error family**: `Error < StandardError` base with `response`/`default_message` (`error.rb:6-22`); HTTP subclasses `BadRequestError`, `ForbiddenError`, `ContextLengthExceededError`, `OverloadedError`, `PaymentRequiredError`, `RateLimitError`, `ServerError`, `ServiceUnavailableError`, `UnauthorizedError` (`error.rb:44-104`); non-HTTP `ConfigurationError`, `ModelNotFoundError`, etc. (`error.rb:25-29`).
- **Per-integration customization**: override `Provider#parse_error(response)` — base at `provider.rb:104-124` (handles Hash/Array/`error.message`/`message`/`detail`); examples: Perplexity HTML (`perplexity.rb:26-40`), OpenRouter nested metadata (`openrouter.rb:27-64`). `Protocol#parse_error` just delegates to the provider (`protocol.rb:82-84`).
- Some typed errors feed retry logic: `Connection#retry_exceptions` retries `RateLimitError`/`ServerError`/`ServiceUnavailableError`/`OverloadedError` (`connection.rb:126-138`).

---

## Step 6 — Every site that must change for a new integration

**Required (runtime visibility):**
1. `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116` — add `RubyLLM::Provider.register :foo, RubyLLM::Providers::Foo` (feeds the `Provider.providers` lookup table consumed at `provider.rb:179-205`, `models.rb:134,153`, `model/info.rb:107`).
2. `lib/ruby_llm.rb:18-34` — add a Zeitwerk inflection **only if** the class name needs non-default casing (e.g. an acronym like `XAI`/`OpenAI`); plain `Foo`/`foo` needs none.
3. `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo.rb` — the new Provider class (**create**).
4. `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo/` — customization modules (**create**, as needed).

**Catalog / discovery:**
5. `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` — model catalog entries (provider field consumed by `Models.find`/`resolve`). Normally generated by the rake task.
6. `lib/ruby_llm/aliases.json` — model aliases (optional; generated by rake).
7. `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb` — `MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP` (`:11-22`) and `PROVIDER_PREFERENCE` (`:26-40`) if the provider participates in models.dev fetching / alias preference.
8. `lib/tasks/models.rake` — `configure_from_env` (`:40-56`) and the rake's own config block (`:43-53`) to add the API key so `rake models:update` can fetch its catalog; alias-generation logic if applicable (`:455+`).

**Test matrix / fixtures:**
9. `spec/support/models_to_test.rb` — add to `chat_models` (`:10-24`) and any relevant matrices (structured output, thinking, vision, embedding…).
10. `spec/support/rubyllm_configuration.rb` — add `config.foo_api_key = ENV.fetch('FOO_API_KEY', 'test')` (`:14-42`).
11. `spec/fixtures/vcr_cassettes/` — recorded HTTP cassettes (dir at `spec/support/vcr_configuration.rb:5`).
12. `spec/ruby_llm/providers/foo_spec.rb` (+ `spec/ruby_llm/providers/foo/` for custom behavior) — mirroring `deep_seek/`, `open_router/`, `perplexity/`, etc.

**Note:** `lib/ruby_llm/configuration.rb` needs **no** edit — accessors are auto-created via `Provider.register`. Docs (`docs/_getting_started/configuration.md`, `docs/index.md`, `docs/_reference/available-models.md`) are non-functional but conventionally updated.

---

## Step 7 — Edit map (assume the new service "Foo" speaks the ChatCompletions dialect)

**CREATE**
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo.rb` — `class Foo < Provider`; inner `class ChatCompletions < Protocols::ChatCompletions; include Foo::Chat; ... end`; `protocol :chat_completions, ChatCompletions`; `api_base` (`@config.foo_api_base || 'https://...'`); `headers` (`Authorization: Bearer #{@config.foo_api_key}`); class methods `capabilities`, `configuration_options %i[foo_api_key foo_api_base]`, `configuration_requirements %i[foo_api_key]`. Model on `xai.rb`/`deepseek.rb`.
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo/chat.rb` — override only what differs (`format_role`, `format_content`, or `render_payload` via `super`), pattern of `xai/chat.rb`/`mistral/chat.rb`.
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo/models.rb` — `parse_list_models_response` if the list endpoint differs (`xai/models.rb`).
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo/capabilities.rb` — if it has its own capability map (`openai/capabilities.rb`); else point `capabilities` at an existing module or omit.
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo/media.rb`, `embeddings.rb` — only if media/embedding shapes differ (`mistral/media.rb`, `mistral/embeddings.rb`).
- `spec/ruby_llm/providers/foo_spec.rb` (+ `spec/ruby_llm/providers/foo/*_spec.rb` for custom error/chat/media behavior).
- `spec/fixtures/vcr_cassettes/...` — recorded cassettes for the new specs.

**CHANGE**
- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116` — add `RubyLLM::Provider.register :foo, RubyLLM::Providers::Foo` (keep alphabetical).
- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:18-34` — Zeitwerk inflection only if the class name needs custom casing.
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:11-22` (`MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP`) and `:26-40` (`PROVIDER_PREFERENCE`) — if participating in models.dev / preference ordering.
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` — add model entries (or run `rake models:update`).
- `lib/ruby_llm/aliases.json` — add aliases if desired.
- `lib/tasks/models.rake:40-56` (`configure_from_env`) and `:43-53` — add `config.foo_api_key = ENV.fetch('FOO_API_KEY', nil)`; alias logic if applicable.
- `spec/support/models_to_test.rb:10-24` (and other matrices) — add `{ provider: :foo, model: '<id>' }`.
- `spec/support/rubyllm_configuration.rb:14-42` — add `config.foo_api_key = ENV.fetch('FOO_API_KEY', 'test')`.
- Docs (optional): `docs/_getting_started/configuration.md`, `docs/index.md`, `docs/_reference/available-models.md`.

**Error handling:** inherited automatically via `ErrorMiddleware` (`connection.rb:117`). Add a `parse_error` override in `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo.rb` only if Foo returns non-standard error bodies (model on `perplexity.rb:26-40` / `openrouter.rb:27-64`).

**Settings:** no `configuration.rb` edit — `Provider.register` (`provider.rb:174-177`) auto-creates `foo_api_key`/`foo_api_base`/`foo_protocol` accessors from `configuration_options`.

**Key architectural split (this is the spine of everything below):**
- **`RubyLLM::Provider`** (`lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:10`) — knows *where* to talk: host/`api_base`, `headers`, auth, config, and *which* protocol to speak. One concrete subclass per external service in `lib/ruby_llm/providers/*.rb` (anthropic, azure, bedrock, deepseek, gemini, gpustack, mistral, ollama, openai, openrouter, perplexity, vertexai, xai), each with a sibling `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<name>/` dir holding its customization modules + `capabilities.rb`.
- **`RubyLLM::Protocol`** (`lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb:7`) — knows *how* to talk: renders payloads, parses responses, streams, endpoints. The wire dialects live in `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/`: `chat_completions` (the OpenAI lingua franca), `responses`, `anthropic`, `gemini`, `converse` (Bedrock) — each a `.rb` class + a dir of mixin modules (chat, media, tools, streaming, models, embeddings…).

**User-facing surface:** `RubyLLM.chat/embed/paint/...` (`lib/ruby_llm.rb:58`) → `RubyLLM::Chat` (`lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:7`). Supporting machinery: `Models` registry (`lib/ruby_llm/models.rb`, catalog `models.json`, aliases `aliases.json`), `Configuration` (`lib/ruby_llm/configuration.rb`), `Connection` (Faraday, `lib/ruby_llm/connection.rb`), `Error`/`ErrorMiddleware` (`lib/ruby_llm/error.rb`, `error_middleware.rb`). Registration is centralized in `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116`; autoloading via Zeitwerk (`lib/ruby_llm.rb:17-39`).

---

## Step 2 — Request → concrete service object

Two phases: **provider selection** (at model binding) and **protocol selection** (at completion).

1. `RubyLLM.chat(...)` → `Chat.new` — `lib/ruby_llm.rb:58` → `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:12`
2. `Chat#initialize` → `with_model` — `chat.rb:20`, defined `chat.rb:84`
3. `with_model` → `Models.resolve(model_id, provider:, assume_exists:, config:)` — `chat.rb:85`
4. `Models.resolve` — `models.rb:132-156`: the lookup.
   - explicit provider → `Provider.providers[provider.to_sym]` (`models.rb:134`)
   - otherwise → `Models.find` then `Provider.providers[model.provider.to_sym]` (`models.rb:152-153`)
   - returns `[model, provider_class.new(config)]` (`models.rb:155`) — **instantiates the concrete Provider object**
5. `Provider#initialize` → `ensure_configured!` + builds `Connection` — `provider.rb:13-17`
6. The lookup table itself: `Provider.providers` hash (`provider.rb:183`), populated by `Provider.register` (`provider.rb:174`), seeded at `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116`. `Model::Info#provider_class` (`model/info.rb:107`) resolves the same way.

Then per request:
7. `chat.ask` → `complete` → `complete_once` → `provider_completion` → `@provider.complete(...)` — `chat.rb:41`, `179`, `278`, `295-310`
8. `Provider#complete` — `provider.rb:54-69`: calls `resolve_protocol(...)` (`provider.rb:56`) then `protocol_class.new(self, model).complete(...)`
9. `resolve_protocol` — `provider.rb:210-213`: `explicit = name || configured_protocol`; explicit → `fetch_protocol`, else `protocol_for(model, **request)`
   - `protocol_for` default → `default_protocol` (`provider.rb:49-51`); e.g. OpenAI overrides to route audio/realtime models (`openai.rb:15`)
   - `fetch_protocol` reads the `protocols` hash (`provider.rb:223`) filled by the class-level `protocol` declaration (`provider.rb:163`, e.g. `openai.rb:7-8`)
10. `Protocol#complete` runs the wire work → `sync_response`/`stream_response` → `completion_url` + `render_payload` + `parse_completion_response` — `protocol.rb:20-44`, `109-114`

## Step 3 — The shared dialect (ChatCompletions) and every customization

**Shared base:** `Protocols::ChatCompletions < Protocol` (`protocols/chat_completions.rb:6`). It `include`s nine modules (`chat_completions.rb:7-15`), each in `protocols/chat_completions/`: `Chat` (`chat.rb` — `completion_url`, `render_payload`, `parse_completion_response`, `format_messages`, `format_role`, token accounting, citations, thinking extraction), `Embeddings`, `Models`, `Moderation`, `Streaming`, `Tools`, `Images`, `Media`, `Transcription`. The base `Protocol` (`protocol.rb`) supplies the `complete/list_models/embed/moderate/paint/transcribe` orchestration. Default temperature normalization is overridden here (`chat_completions.rb:17`).

**Customization mechanism:** a provider declares an inner `class ChatCompletions < Protocols::ChatCompletions` and `include`s provider modules that override the `module_function` methods; plus provider-level overrides of `api_base`, `headers`, `parse_error`, `protocol_for`, and class methods `capabilities`/`configuration_*`/`local?`.

**Every existing ChatCompletions integration and how it differs:**

| Provider | File | Customizations |
|---|---|---|
| **OpenAI** | `openai.rb` | Canonical. Also offers `Protocols::Responses` (`:7-8`); `protocol_for` routes `audio/realtime/search-preview` models to chat_completions (`:15-17`); `OpenAI::Capabilities`. |
| **DeepSeek** | `deepseek.rb:8-12` | `DeepSeek::Chat` (`deepseek/chat.rb`): `format_role` (no `developer` role), `format_content` (text-only, no docs/images/audio). `DeepSeek::Capabilities`. |
| **Perplexity** | `perplexity.rb:8-11` | `Perplexity::Chat`, `Perplexity::Models`, `perplexity/media.rb`; **provider-level `parse_error` override** for HTML auth errors (`perplexity.rb:26-40`). |
| **xAI** | `xai.rb:8-11` | `XAI::Chat` (`format_role`, image-only `format_content` — `xai/chat.rb`), `XAI::Models` (custom `parse_list_models_response` — `xai/models.rb`). |
| **Mistral** | `mistral.rb:8-13` | `Mistral::Chat` (overrides `render_payload` via `super` — deletes `stream_options`, configures thinking, normalizes `tool_choice` `any`/required — `mistral/chat.rb:26-52`), `Mistral::Embeddings` (custom URL/payload/parse), `Mistral::Media` (`document_url`), `Mistral::Models`; `Mistral::Capabilities`. |
| **Ollama** | `ollama.rb:8-12` | `Ollama::Chat`, `Media`, `Models`; `local? = true` (`:35`); `api_base` required, key optional. |
| **OpenRouter** | `openrouter.rb:8-13` | `OpenRouter::Chat`, `Images`, `Models`, **`Streaming`**; **provider-level `parse_error`** for nested `error.metadata.raw` (`openrouter.rb:27-64`). |
| **GPUStack** | `gpustack.rb:8-9` | Subclasses **`Ollama::ChatCompletions`** (not the base), adds `GPUStack::Models`; `local? = true`. |

**Customization points to know (a missed one = silently unsupported):** `format_role`, `format_content`/`Media`, `render_payload` (super+tweak), `build_tool_choice`, `parse_completion_response`, `parse_list_models_response` (Models), embeddings trio (`embedding_url`/`render_embedding_payload`/`parse_embedding_response`), `Streaming`, `Images`, and provider-level `api_base`/`headers`/`parse_error`/`protocol_for`.

## Step 4 — Settings: declare → register → validate

- **Declare** (per provider class): `configuration_options` (all keys to expose) and `configuration_requirements` (must-be-present keys). E.g. `openai.rb:32-44`, `deepseek.rb:29-35`, `ollama.rb:27-33`.
- **Register**: `Provider.register(name, class)` (`provider.rb:174-177`) calls `RubyLLM::Configuration.register_provider_options(provider_class.configuration_options + [:"#{name}_protocol"])`. → `register_provider_options` (`configuration.rb:25-27`) → `option` (`configuration.rb:10-23`) which defines an `attr_reader` + a blank-stripping writer on `Configuration`. So **registering the provider auto-creates its config accessors** — no manual edit of `configuration.rb`.
- **Validate**:
  - `Provider.configured?(config)` (`provider.rb:159-161`) = all requirements truthy.
  - `Provider#ensure_configured!` (`provider.rb:237-249`) runs at instantiation (`provider.rb:15`) and raises `ConfigurationError` with a generated config snippet if any requirement is missing.
  - `Provider#configured?` (`provider.rb:92-94`); fleet-level filters `configured_providers`/`configured_remote_providers` (`provider.rb:195-205`).

---

## Step 5 — HTTP failures → typed errors

- **Wired** in Faraday stack: `faraday.use :llm_errors, provider: @provider` — `connection.rb:117`; middleware registered at `error_middleware.rb:90`.
- **Entry**: `ErrorMiddleware#call` → `on_complete` → `self.class.parse_error(provider:, response:)` — `error_middleware.rb:14-18`.
- **Classification**: `ErrorMiddleware.parse_error` — `error_middleware.rb:41-71`:
  1. `message = provider&.parse_error(response)` (`:42`) — provider extracts the human message.
  2. `case response.status` maps codes → typed errors: 400→`BadRequestError`/`ContextLengthExceededError`, 401→`UnauthorizedError`, 402→`PaymentRequiredError`, 403→`ForbiddenError`, 429→`RateLimitError`/`ContextLengthExceededError`, 500→`ServerError`, 502-504→`ServiceUnavailableError`, 529→`OverloadedError`, else→`Error` (`:44-70`).
  - Heuristics `context_length_exceeded?`/`rate_limited?` via regex tables (`error_middleware.rb:21-39`, `75-85`).
- **Error family**: `Error < StandardError` base with `response`/`default_message` (`error.rb:6-22`); HTTP subclasses `BadRequestError`, `ForbiddenError`, `ContextLengthExceededError`, `OverloadedError`, `PaymentRequiredError`, `RateLimitError`, `ServerError`, `ServiceUnavailableError`, `UnauthorizedError` (`error.rb:44-104`); non-HTTP `ConfigurationError`, `ModelNotFoundError`, etc. (`error.rb:25-29`).
- **Per-integration customization**: override `Provider#parse_error(response)` — base at `provider.rb:104-124` (handles Hash/Array/`error.message`/`message`/`detail`); examples: Perplexity HTML (`perplexity.rb:26-40`), OpenRouter nested metadata (`openrouter.rb:27-64`). `Protocol#parse_error` just delegates to the provider (`protocol.rb:82-84`).
- Some typed errors feed retry logic: `Connection#retry_exceptions` retries `RateLimitError`/`ServerError`/`ServiceUnavailableError`/`OverloadedError` (`connection.rb:126-138`).

## Step 6 — Every site that must change for a new integration

**Required (runtime visibility):**
1. `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116` — add `RubyLLM::Provider.register :foo, RubyLLM::Providers::Foo` (feeds the `Provider.providers` lookup table consumed at `provider.rb:179-205`, `models.rb:134,153`, `model/info.rb:107`).
2. `lib/ruby_llm.rb:18-34` — add a Zeitwerk inflection **only if** the class name needs non-default casing (e.g. an acronym like `XAI`/`OpenAI`); plain `Foo`/`foo` needs none.
3. `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo.rb` — the new Provider class (**create**).
4. `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo/` — customization modules (**create**, as needed).

**Catalog / discovery:**
5. `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` — model catalog entries (provider field consumed by `Models.find`/`resolve`). Normally generated by the rake task.
6. `lib/ruby_llm/aliases.json` — model aliases (optional; generated by rake).
7. `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb` — `MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP` (`:11-22`) and `PROVIDER_PREFERENCE` (`:26-40`) if the provider participates in models.dev fetching / alias preference.
8. `lib/tasks/models.rake` — `configure_from_env` (`:40-56`) and the rake's own config block (`:43-53`) to add the API key so `rake models:update` can fetch its catalog; alias-generation logic if applicable (`:455+`).

**Test matrix / fixtures:**
9. `spec/support/models_to_test.rb` — add to `chat_models` (`:10-24`) and any relevant matrices (structured output, thinking, vision, embedding…).
10. `spec/support/rubyllm_configuration.rb` — add `config.foo_api_key = ENV.fetch('FOO_API_KEY', 'test')` (`:14-42`).
11. `spec/fixtures/vcr_cassettes/` — recorded HTTP cassettes (dir at `spec/support/vcr_configuration.rb:5`).
12. `spec/ruby_llm/providers/foo_spec.rb` (+ `spec/ruby_llm/providers/foo/` for custom behavior) — mirroring `deep_seek/`, `open_router/`, `perplexity/`, etc.

**Note:** `lib/ruby_llm/configuration.rb` needs **no** edit — accessors are auto-created via `Provider.register`. Docs (`docs/_getting_started/configuration.md`, `docs/index.md`, `docs/_reference/available-models.md`) are non-functional but conventionally updated.

## Step 7 — Edit map (assume the new service "Foo" speaks the ChatCompletions dialect)

**CREATE**
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo.rb` — `class Foo < Provider`; inner `class ChatCompletions < Protocols::ChatCompletions; include Foo::Chat; ... end`; `protocol :chat_completions, ChatCompletions`; `api_base` (`@config.foo_api_base || 'https://...'`); `headers` (`Authorization: Bearer #{@config.foo_api_key}`); class methods `capabilities`, `configuration_options %i[foo_api_key foo_api_base]`, `configuration_requirements %i[foo_api_key]`. Model on `xai.rb`/`deepseek.rb`.
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo/chat.rb` — override only what differs (`format_role`, `format_content`, or `render_payload` via `super`), pattern of `xai/chat.rb`/`mistral/chat.rb`.
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo/models.rb` — `parse_list_models_response` if the list endpoint differs (`xai/models.rb`).
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo/capabilities.rb` — if it has its own capability map (`openai/capabilities.rb`); else point `capabilities` at an existing module or omit.
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo/media.rb`, `embeddings.rb` — only if media/embedding shapes differ (`mistral/media.rb`, `mistral/embeddings.rb`).
- `spec/ruby_llm/providers/foo_spec.rb` (+ `spec/ruby_llm/providers/foo/*_spec.rb` for custom error/chat/media behavior).
- `spec/fixtures/vcr_cassettes/...` — recorded cassettes for the new specs.

**CHANGE**
- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116` — add `RubyLLM::Provider.register :foo, RubyLLM::Providers::Foo` (keep alphabetical).
- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:18-34` — Zeitwerk inflection only if the class name needs custom casing.
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:11-22` (`MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP`) and `:26-40` (`PROVIDER_PREFERENCE`) — if participating in models.dev / preference ordering.
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` — add model entries (or run `rake models:update`).
- `lib/ruby_llm/aliases.json` — add aliases if desired.
- `lib/tasks/models.rake:40-56` (`configure_from_env`) and `:43-53` — add `config.foo_api_key = ENV.fetch('FOO_API_KEY', nil)`; alias logic if applicable.
- `spec/support/models_to_test.rb:10-24` (and other matrices) — add `{ provider: :foo, model: '<id>' }`.
- `spec/support/rubyllm_configuration.rb:14-42` — add `config.foo_api_key = ENV.fetch('FOO_API_KEY', 'test')`.
- Docs (optional): `docs/_getting_started/configuration.md`, `docs/index.md`, `docs/_reference/available-models.md`.

**Error handling:** inherited automatically via `ErrorMiddleware` (`connection.rb:117`). Add a `parse_error` override in `lib/ruby_llm/providers/foo.rb` only if Foo returns non-standard error bodies (model on `perplexity.rb:26-40` / `openrouter.rb:27-64`).

**Settings:** no `configuration.rb` edit — `Provider.register` (`provider.rb:174-177`) auto-creates `foo_api_key`/`foo_api_base`/`foo_protocol` accessors from `configuration_options`.
